issuesFixed.description: Replaced PHP native date() with wp_date() for timezone-aware date comparison
changes: Line 46: date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) === date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) → wp_date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) === wp_date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) (all-day same-day check)
Line 57: date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) === date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) → wp_date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) === wp_date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) (timed same-day check)

issuesFixed.description: Wrapped date/time display in <time datetime='...'> elements with ISO 8601 datetime attributes in both PHP and TSX
changes:
PHP: Added $date_time_iso variable built with wp_date(). All-day single: 'Y-m-d'. All-day range: 'Y-m-d/Y-m-d'. Timed: 'Y-m-d\TH:i'. Timed range: 'Y-m-d\TH:i/Y-m-d\TH:i'. Wrapped

content in element.
TSX: Added buildIsoDatetime() helper with same logic using local Date methods. Added dateTimeIso computed value. Wrapped dateTimeDisplay in <time dateTime={dateTimeIso || undefined}> element.

warnings: Pre-existing useSelect dependency warnings (same as PostHero — not block-specific)

warningDetails: Root wrapper element mismatch (known false positive — same as PostHero)

notesOnImplementation: ISO datetime uses interval notation (start/end) for range events, e.g. '2026-02-20T18:00/2026-02-22T21:00'. This is valid per HTML spec for time ranges.
All-day events use date-only format ('2026-02-20') per HTML spec for dates without times.

The element is placed inside the existing

wrapper so display styling is unchanged.
Prettier auto-formatted the two new single-line arrow functions in buildIsoDatetime() — run bun run format was used to fix the formatting errors.
